prompt: Create a movie production-grade character design sheet for directors, casting teams, and costume departments. It must feel like a high-budget animated film pitch board, not a generic model sheet. Core instructions (non-negotiable): No generic layouts, no uniform grids, no symmetry; composition requires art direction and asymmetry. Character Identity: name, alias, age, height, body type, ethnicity/design language. Facial Design: Structure, skin, eyes, hair, prominent features. Psychological Profile: core traits, internal conflicts, behavioral patterns, emotional baseline. Acting Guidance: Must feel like a real actor, micro-expressions, body language, idle behaviors. Clothing and Textures: main garments, footwear, accessories, props, material accuracy. Turnaround Requirements: Front, 3/4 view, side, back; dynamic angles must remain consistent. Head Research: Expressive head variations, neutral/3/4/profile/downward/upward/dynamic. Cinematic Portrait: Environment, lighting, tone, expression, camera specifications. Output Quality: Extremely high detail, sharp focus, production-ready fidelity.
